{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

::: notes
Each entry gives the derivative of the **column quantity**
with respect to the **row quantity**;
that is, the entry in row $i$ and column $j$ is
$\derivf{(\text{column } j \text{ quantity})}{(\text{row } i \text{ quantity})}$.

The coefficient $\b$ here refers to a single scalar predictor;
for a vector $\vb$ of coefficients,
replace $x$ with $\vx$ and treat each entry as a dot product with $\vx$.
:::

:::{#tbl-logistic-deriv-matrix}

$$
\begin{array}{r|cccc}
 & \prob & \odds & \logodds & \b \\
\hline
\prob
  & 1
  & \dfrac{1}{\sqf{1-\prob}}
  & \dfrac{1}{\prob(1-\prob)}
  & \dfrac{1}{x\,\prob(1-\prob)} \\[10pt]
\odds
  & \sqf{1-\prob}
  & 1
  & \dfrac{1}{\odds}
  & \dfrac{1}{x\,\odds} \\[10pt]
\logodds
  & \prob(1-\prob)
  & \odds
  & 1
  & \dfrac{1}{x} \\[10pt]
\b
  & x\,\prob(1-\prob)
  & x\,\odds
  & x
  & 1 \\
\end{array}
$$

Matrix of logistic regression derivatives.
The row labels are the denominator variable
and the column labels are the numerator variable.
:::
